title: Security Zone Definition
description: Static-ish registry record for ONE ground (or space) zone's security configuration: the admin-set base tier on the safe-zone <-> consensual-PvP gradient, room-level faction overrides, and lawless incentives. The Director applies a TRANSIENT overlay computed from faction_zone_state influence + active events; this record holds only the durable base the overlay never overwrites. Grounds security_zones_design_v1.

source.source_policy: Design schema. security_zones_design_v1 (EVE-style high/low/null adapted) supplies the three-tier model as reference; recast Clone Wars era (Republic authority, not Imperial). The effective-security function is server-authoritative and the single gate every combat initiation routes through.

domain.description: Space zones mirror the model: dock=secured, orbit/lane=contested, deep_space=lawless.

security_base.description: Admin-set base tier. secured = no combat (safe zone, new-player ground); contested = PvE on, PvP by mutual consent (challenge/accept, bounty, territory contest); lawless = unrestricted PvP. This is the consensual-PvP gradient. Director overlays may shift the EFFECTIVE tier transiently but never change this value.

faction_overrides.description: Room-level overrides inside an otherwise-secured zone (e.g. a Republic garrison interior is effectively lawless for CIS/hostile-standing players). Checked first in the effective-security function.

faction_axis.description: Owning faction. Players hostile/unfriendly to it get a downgraded effective tier in this node.

overlay_rules.description: Declarative thresholds the Director uses to compute the transient effective-security overlay from faction_zone_state influence + active events. Owner-tunable; defaults from security_zones_design_v1.

hutt_surge_downgrade_at.description: Hutt (criminal) influence >= this downgrades effective security one tier.

crackdown_upgrades_contested.description: An active republic_crackdown event upgrades contested -> secured for the duration.

lawless_incentives.description: Why players risk lawless zones (security_zones_design_v1 §7). Data hooks only; the CP-rate bonus interacts with the OPEN OWNER DECISION on CP pace and must not be baked here.

cp_rate_bonus_flag.description: Whether a lawless CP-tick bonus applies. The MAGNITUDE/whether-at-all is an OPEN OWNER DECISION (CP progression pace) - this is the on/off hook only.

claimable.description: Whether orgs may claim nodes here (contested + lawless only).
